old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because ceramic vessel ancestry belongs to fermentation and distillation apparatus upstream, not to the scoped act of making spirits from fermented alcohol.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines distilled spirits by distilling fermented agricultural ethanol; it does not make pottery a separate prerequisite of the beverage-spirit category.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports fermented ethanol plus distillation, not pottery as a direct spirits dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Pottery remains available upstream through fermentation and distillation histories; keeping it direct would overstate a generic container technology.

ontology_before: Distillation for Alcoholic Spirits depended directly on Pottery in addition to fermentation and distillation.
ontology_after: Distillation for Alcoholic Spirits depends on fermentation and distillation; pottery remains upstream where those technologies require vessels.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pottery is no longer a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to ceramic-still distillation specifically.
fermentation_basic and distillation_advanced lose their vessel/container ancestry.
A stronger source shows early alcoholic-spirit production was defined by a pottery-specific method.
